items.title: JPJ to unleash drones nationwide during Aidiladha ops, keeping watch on 288 accident hotspots
items.summary: DENGKIL, May 23 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) will maximise the use of drones during Ops Hari Raya Aidil Adha (Ops HRAA), which will be carried out from May 24 to 31. JPJ deputy director-general (Planning and Operations) Datuk Jazmanie Shafawi said that six drones would be deployed at hotspot areas to enhance the detection of various traffic offences. Jazmanie said the use of drones was first introduced during the previous Hari Raya Aidilfitri operation, resulting in JPJ issuing 5,000 notices under Section 114 of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333).

items.title: Fire and Rescue Dept bids farewell to veteran officer Ahmad Izram
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: After more than three decades in uniform, Fire and Rescue Department deputy director-general (Operations) Datuk Ahmad Izram Osman bade farewell to the service today, leaving behind a decorated legacy marked by operational leadership, innovation and hopes for a stronger, more technologically advanced department. Ahmad Izram, who officially retires on May 24 after 34 years and five months in service, said he hoped the department would continue progressing and expanding key innovations, particularly the Command, Control, Communication, Computer and Intelligence (C4i) system nationwide. Ahmad Izram also expressed hope that the department would continue advancing beyond its current achievements under future leadership.

items.title: Prominent lawyer mourns son killed as Volkswagen collides with cement lorry on Karak Highway
items.summary: Datuk Akberdin Abdul Kader, 62, a prominent lawyer, said his son Adli Hakim Akberdin, 24, a student at the International Islamic University of Malaysia (IIUM), had planned to marry his close friend Athirah Najwa Mohd Tajul Ashikin, 27, who was also seriously injured in the incident. “The girl who was badly injured (Athirah Najwa) was supposed to be my son’s future wife, and we had already planned for them to get married next year,” he said when met at the Section 21 Muslim Cemetery here yesterday. Meanwhile, Akberdin said Adli Hakin, his fifth child, was a good-natured, obedient son who was well-liked by family members.

items.title: Pentagon publishes new UFO records including reports of 'green orbs' and discs
items.summary: WSHINGTON: The US Defence Department on Friday released a second batch of previously classified files on alleged UFO sightings, including references to people reporting unexplained green orbs, discs and fireballs. "This file contains 209 sightings of 'green orbs', 'discs', and 'fireballs' reported near the military base," the Defence Department said. Experts said the first batch contained new videos of known sightings but gave no conclusive evidence of alien technology or extraterrestrial life.

items.title: #NSTviral: Bakery's order-calling system leaves customers stressed and impressed [WATCH]
items.summary: The video captures rows of bakery boxes meticulously lined up as a worker distributes them by loudly and clearly announcing order numbers with rhythmic precision. Rather than the usual frantic scramble seen at popular eateries, the crowd appeared calm and entertained by the worker's thorough and authoritative distribution style. One netizen noted that the worker's delivery was so intense it felt more like a grand announcement than a simple bread pickup.

items.title: When Jakarta moves the palm oil export gate
items.summary: If exporters must keep earnings in Indonesian state-owned banks, export policy becomes currency management and national signaling too. A central export body for palm oil may sound neat and decisive. First, export freedom may narrow.

items.title: PM Anwar tells forum Malaysia must embrace risk in AI and digital ventures to secure future growth
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said losses alone are not the main concern if the investments are critical for the country’s future economic development. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan, Securities Commissioner Malaysia chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi and Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir were present at the event. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the government is taking major efforts to combat corruption and strengthen governance to address long-standing structural issues.

items.title: PAS’ Tuan Ibrahim says Bersatu actions weakening Perikatan, warns against repeating history
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — PAS deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man has publicly rebuked Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia over a series of recent actions he said had weakened Perikatan Nasional (PN) and disrupted the administration of several Opposition-led states. According to The New Straits Times , Tuan Ibrahim said political alliances must be anchored on trust and solidarity, warning that recent developments involving Bersatu were straining ties within the coalition ahead of the 16th general election. Tuan Ibrahim also appeared to caution Bersatu against escalating internal disciplinary actions that could further fracture cooperation within PN.

items.title: The 'Skye' Is The Limit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The arrival of a fabless designer on Bursa Malaysia reflects growing confidence in the country's ambition to move from the downstream to the upstream of the global semiconductor value chain. SkyeChip Bhd's Main Market debut was a striking one. Domestic Patient Capital SkyeChip's listing was anchored by 22 cornerstone investors who together took up close to 60 per cent of the institutional offering.

items.title: Trump's intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard resigns
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard announced her resignation, ending the tenure of a controversial pick who appeared to be at odds with President Donald Trump over the Iran war. "He faces major challenges in the coming weeks and months," Gabbard said in a letter to Trump that she posted on X. Trump hailed Gabbard, whose job involved coordinating information from the sprawling network of 18 US intelligence agencies for the president's daily briefing.

items.title: Home Ministry pushes MyNIISe rollout, touts safer, quicker immigration services at your fingertips
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — Malaysians are encouraged to download the National Integrated Immigration System (MyNIISe) application and complete their registration to enjoy more efficient and seamless digital immigration services. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the application was introduced to enable various key processes to be carried out directly via smartphones without the need to go through complicated procedures. “The MyNIISe application was introduced to provide a smoother, faster and more user-friendly travel and immigration experience,” he said in a Facebook post yesterday.

items.title: Layered shareholding structure: Indirect path for high-profile acquisitions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tan Sri Syed Mokhtar Al-Bukhary's move to buy a major stake in Eco World Development Group Bhd is more than just another corporate deal. It is a textbook example of how share acquisition structuring, or layered shareholding structure, is used to secure strategic control through indirect ownership, industry observers said. The transaction also gives him control over a 3.28 per cent stake in Eco World International Bhd through EWI Capital Bhd.

items.title: PAS issues directive barring unauthorised comments on party developments, urges members to remain calm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 23 — PAS has instructed its leaders and members not to comment publicly on recent developments involving the Islamist party without prior approval from its top leadership. Its secretary-general, Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, said the directive applies across all media and communication platforms, including social media, to prevent confusion and misinterpretation. “PAS requests that all PAS leaders and members refrain from making any statements or comments regarding the latest developments involving PAS and other parties through any media or communication channels unless with special permission from the party,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Police nab six over murder of youth in Pekan Nanas
items.summary: PONTIAN: Police arrested six suspects within 24 hours following the murder of a youth in front of an eatery in Pekan Nanas at 3.53am on Thursday. Pontian district police chief, Superintendent Hadzrat Hussein Mion Hussain, said the suspects were tracked down and arrested through an operation involving the Johor contingent police criminal task force and the criminal investigation division of the Pontian district police headquarters. Hadzrat said police also seized two vehicles, namely a Nissan Navara and a Toyota Estima.

items.title: Berjaya Food remains in the red with RM14mil quarterly loss
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Food Bhd posted another quarterly loss as costs and expenses continued to outpace modest revenue growth. However, the loss was lower compared with RM37.19 million recorded in the third quarter of the 2025 financial year, following cost-control measures and store closures. Nevertheless, Berjaya Food Bhd remained optimistic about its prospects, saying recent efforts had begun to show positive results.

items.title: US weighs new Iran strikes as Trump signals urgency in Washington
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States is weighing new military strikes on Iran, US media outlets reported on Friday. The reports, from CBS and Axios, come just hours after US President Donald Trump said he would not travel to attend his son's wedding this weekend due to "circumstances pertaining to government" and his "love for the United States of America." Both Axios and CBS said that a final decision on new strikes hasn't been made.

items.title: Johor polls is up to Sultan Ibrahim, says BN deputy chairman
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said any decision to dissolve the Johor Legislative Assembly for a snap election rested entirely with His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, amid growing speculation over early state polls. He said Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi would advise the ruler on the appropriate timing for the election. Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, said he was confident Onn Hafiz would consult the party's central leadership once royal consent was obtained.

items.title: Download, register on MyNIISe for smoother immigration services: Saifuddin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysians are encouraged to download the National Integrated Immigration System (MyNIISe) application and complete their registration to enjoy more efficient and seamless digital immigration services. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the application was introduced to enable key processes to be carried out directly via smartphones without the need for complicated procedures. "The MyNIISe application was introduced to provide a smoother, faster and more user‑friendly travel and immigration experience," he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Jakim turns to AI to speed up halal certification process
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) is targeting a shorter processing period for halal certification applications through the use of artificial intelligence (AI) technology. Director‑general Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee said the move will be realised through the introduction of the MyeHALAL 2.0 system, supported by the MyHALALINGREDIENTS database, within the next few months. Apart from the use of AI, Sirajuddin said Jakim had also introduced a whitelist initiative for companies with a high level of commitment and strong compliance records to further expedite the halal certification approval process.

items.title: PM: CyberSecurity Centre benefits 10,000 civil servants, ICT staff
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia CyberSecurity Centre of Excellence (CCoE) in Cyberjaya has benefited nearly 10,000 civil servants and ICT professionals through various courses and certifications since 2024, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the initiative has strengthened the country's preparedness amid increasingly complex cyber threats and post‑quantum challenges. "This was to discuss efforts to strengthen local competencies in cybersecurity and future technologies," he said.

items.title: Tuan Ibrahim: Bersatu's moves are 'weakening' opposition pact
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man pulled no punches in his assessment of Perikatan Nasional (PN) component Bersatu's recent actions, saying they appeared to be weakening the opposition pact. He said political cooperation must be built on trust and camaraderie, with efforts focused on strengthening alliances. "Political cooperation must be built on trust and solidarity.

items.title: MCMC says new regulatory codes will improve online safety for children [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has introduced two new regulatory codes under the Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA), in a move aimed at strengthening protection for minors against harmful online content. It said the framework placed greater responsibility on online platforms to address digital harms, particularly in safeguarding children and vulnerable users through an outcome-based approach that allows flexibility in how safety measures are implemented. LeapEd Services' project lead on child protection and safeguarding, Rajveen Kaur, said the regulatory move reflected an encouraging shift towards more structured protection for children online.

items.title: 40,000 told to flee as California toxic chemical tank leak sparks explosion fears
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: Tens of thousands of people were ordered to leave their homes in California Friday after a huge chemical tank began leaking, with warnings it might blow up, sending toxic fumes over a heavily populated area. The tank contains 26,000 litres of methyl methacrylate, a volatile and flammable liquid used to make plastics, with firefighters warning things were looking bad. "There are literally two options left," Incident Commander Craig Covey said.

items.title: Pakistan army chief visits Teheran as Trump pushes for Iran deal
items.summary: TEHERAN: Pakistan's powerful army chief arrived in Teheran on Friday as diplomacy around the Middle East war gathered pace, with Iran weighing a new US peace proposal while warning that deep divisions still stand in the way of a deal. The disagreements between Iran and the United States remained "deep and extensive", he added, according to Iran's ISNA news agency. Baqaei said a Qatari delegation also met Iran's foreign minister Friday.

items.title: Israeli air raids kill 10 in Lebanon as cross-border fighting escalates
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel staged fresh airstrikes in Lebanon on Saturday after earlier raids killed 10 people, according to media and the government in Beirut, targeting an area near the Syrian border. - EXCHANGES OF FIRE - Lebanon's health ministry said Israeli strikes killed 10 people on Friday, including six rescuers and a child in continued exchanges of fire. Separately, the Israeli military said early on Friday morning that it had killed two people close to the border.

items.title: US Green Card seekers must now apply from home countries
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration announced on Friday that most foreigners seeking a Green Card granting permanent US residency must apply from their home countries. "From now on, an alien who is in the US temporarily and wants a Green Card must return to their home country to apply, except in extraordinary circumstances," US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) spokesman Zach Kahler said in a statement. He said Green Card applications would be handled by the State Department at US consular offices abroad.

items.title: Four dead, 90 trapped in China coal mine accident
items.summary: BEIJING: Four workers died and 90 were trapped underground at a coal mine in northern China after carbon monoxide reached dangerous levels at the site, the official Xinhua News Agency reported early Saturday. A total of 247 miners were underground at the Liushenyu coal mine in Shanxi Province when the accident occurred Friday night, the agency said. Shanxi Province is the coal-mining capital of China.

items.title: Johor police bust RM16.4mil drug lab, vape syndicate
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Police have busted a drug trafficking syndicate following the discovery of an illegal laboratory and a vape cartridge processing centre containing drugs worth RM16.4 million in three separate raids around Iskandar Puteri and Johor Baru on May 18. Johor acting police chief deputy superintendent said that in the 5.30pm operation, four suspects, including two foreigners aged between 27 and 60, were arrested following intelligence from the Johor police Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department and the Iskandar Puteri district Narcotics Crime Investigation Division. "Police seized six plastic bottles and 32 cartridges containing liquid suspected to be methamphetamine (3.48 litres), worth RM522,000, as well as nine containers of the chemical etomidate (8.46kg), worth RM1.269 million," he said at a press conference at the Iskandar Puteri district police headquarters yesterday.

items.title: RTD to deploy drones around the clock for Aidiladha traffic operation
items.summary: DENGKIL: The Road Transport Department (RTD) will maximise the use of drones during Op Hari Raya Aidiladha (Op HRAA), which will be carried out from May 24 to 31. RTD deputy director-general (Planning and Operations) Datuk Jazmanie Shafawi said that six drones would be deployed at hotspot areas to enhance the detection of various traffic offences. Jazmanie said the use of drones was first introduced during the previous Hari Raya Aidilfitri operation, resulting in the RTD issuing 5,000 notices under Section 114 of the Road Transport Act.

items.title: Over 169,000 SPM school leavers secure public universities places through UPUOnline
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A total of 169,803 students who sat for the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination have been offered places at public higher education institutions through UPUOnline. The Higher Education Ministry said of the total, 91,357 candidates received offers from public universities, 60,947 from polytechnics, 15,844 from community colleges and 1,655 from institutions under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Higher Education Division. "A total of 363 programmes were offered for this intake, comprising 41 certificate programmes, 57 foundation programmes, 264 diploma programmes and one bachelor's degree programme.

items.title: UN top court says 'right to strike' protected under key labour treaty
items.summary: AMSTERDAM: The right to strike is internationally protected under a key labour treaty, according to an advisory legal opinion by the International Court of Justice (ICJ), the UN's top judges in The Hague said on Thursday, reported German news agency dpa. The protection falls under the International Labour Organisation's (ILO) 1948 convention on freedom of association, the court said. In a ruling, Germany's Federal Labour Court said employees' demands without the right to strike would amount to "collective begging".

items.title: Palestine Presidency condemns Israeli minister's treatment of flotilla activists
items.summary: RAMALLAH: The Palestinian Presidency on Friday condemned Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir and occupation forces' treatment of the abducted Gaza-bound Global Sumud flotilla activists. The Presidency affirmed that intercepting the flotilla vessels in international waters not only constitutes an illegal act, but also amounts to an act of maritime piracy, and a flagrant violation of international law and international humanitarian law, Palestinian news agency reported. The action was a blatant assault on freedom of navigation and international humanitarian and solidarity efforts, it added.

items.title: China to crack down on 'illegal' cross-border securities
items.summary: SHANGHAI/HONG KONG: China announced a major crackdown on cross-border investment on Friday and said it would punish brokers it accused of illegally moving money to foreign markets, sending their shares plunging. Online brokers Tiger, Futu and Longbridge would be penalised for soliciting business in China without an onshore licence, the securities regulator said. It said illegal gains would be forfeited.

items.title: 'PKR has changed': Sabah leader quits party after nearly three decades
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sabah PKR State Leadership Council vice-chairman Mohd Amin Abdul Mem has announced his resignation from all party posts and his departure from PKR, ending his 27-year involvement with the reformasi movement. Mohd Amin, who is also the Semporna PKR branch chief, said the decision was made after evaluating the current state of the party, which he claimed has veered away from the original principles of the 'reformasi' fight. However, Mohd Amin added, PKR has changed since the unity government was formed, saying that it has become a culture of compromise and intense 'politicking' increasingly evident within the party.

items.title: Pakistan military chief departs to Iran: security sources
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir departed on Friday for an official visit to Teheran, security sources told AFP, as Iran examined the latest US proposal to end the Middle East war. Munir's visit comes after Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi travelled to Iran on Wednesday and met President Masoud Pezeshkian and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi. Pakistan hosted in April the only direct negotiations between US and Iranian officials to take place since the war began.

items.title: Man killed in Karak highway crash was set to marry next year
items.summary: Datuk Akberdin Abdul Kader, 62, a prominent lawyer, said his son Adli Hakim Akberdin, 24, a student at the International Islamic University of Malaysia (IIUM), had planned to marry his close friend Athirah Najwa Mohd Tajul Ashikin, 27, who was also seriously injured in the incident. "The girl who was badly injured (Athirah Najwa) was supposed to be my son's future wife, and we had already planned for them to get married next year," he said when met at the Section 21 Muslim Cemetery here today. Meanwhile, Akberdin said Adli Hakim, his fifth child, was a good-natured, obedient son who was well-liked by family members.

items.title: Man in Bukit Kemuning teen assault case pleads guilty to sexual assault, wrongful confinement and robbery
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 22 — A debt collector who became the subject of a police manhunt over a sexual assault case involving a teenage boy pleaded guilty in separate courts here today to a string of offences, including physical sexual assault, wrongful confinement, causing hurt, robbery and drug use. Mohamad Fadwan Abd Muaain, 42, had pleaded guilty before Sessions Court judge Rasyihah Ghazali to a charge of physically sexually assaulting a 14-year-old boy at an apartment in Section 32 here between 8.05am and 11.50am on May 11. In a separate Magistrate’s Court, Mohamad Fadwan was sentenced to a total of 15 months’ imprisonment by Magistrate Wan Noora Nisa Ngadrin after pleading guilty to wrongfully confining and causing injury to the same teenager.

items.title: State government moves to strengthen public transport ecosystem ahead of RTS debut
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 22 — RTS Operations Pte Ltd (RTSO) has described the Johor state government’s proactive and comprehensive plan to strengthen public transport ecosystem integration as a timely move ahead of the operationalisation of the Johor Bahru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link next year. RTSO chairman Datuk Khairil Anwar Ahmad said the initiative, which includes improvements to pedestrian connectivity, would help facilitate smooth passenger movement. He said the collaboration would include supporting more comprehensive mobility planning through operational observations and passenger movement trends once the RTS Link begins operations.

items.title: Govt task force on agency reform to prioritise local expertise, speed up public‑service digitalisation, says Shamsul Azri
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 —The Special Task Force on Agency Reform (STAR) has agreed to prioritise local think tanks and consultants in advisory roles to the government, while accelerating digitalisation initiatives in public services, said Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar. “Amid global economic uncertainty, STAR is seeking solutions to strengthen the implementation of government strategies,” he said. In a separate Facebook post, Shamsul Azri said he also met industry representatives and chambers of commerce through the Special Task Force to Facilitate Business (PEMUDAH) after the STAR meeting.

items.title: Religious affairs minister urges Muslims, Malaysians to embrace spirit of Rukun Negara and respect royal institution
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan has called on Muslims and Malaysians to continue appreciating the spirit of the Rukun Negara, while respecting the royal institution and the nation’s unity. Recently, Sultan Sharafuddin expressed hope that members of the public, especially the younger generation visiting Dataran Selangor in Shah Alam, where the Rukun Negara Monument Plaque is located, would read and appreciate the principles of the Rukun Negara as the foundation of unity among Malaysians. His Royal Highness also called on all Selangor state assemblymen to visit Dataran Selangor twice a month to read and understand the meaning of the Rukun Negara inscribed on the monument plaque.

items.title: Selangor to expand Selgate facilities to strengthen public health ecosystem, says Amirudin
items.summary: RAWANG, May 22 — The construction of a network of hospitals under the Selgate Healthcare (SELGATE) group is a creative strategy based on ‘entrepreneur state’ to strengthen an inclusive and sustainable public health ecosystem in Selangor. Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the official opening of Selgate Specialist Hospital Rawang (SSH Rawang) today is able to meet the demand for specialist services in the northern corridor of Selangor which is currently recording rapid population growth. He said the 224-bed hospital would not only meet the needs of private healthcare, but also be part of the state government’s efforts to expand health benefits to the people.

items.title: TikTok works with MCMC to tighten platform safety and protect young users in Malaysia
items.summary: KUCHING, May 22 — Global short-video platform TikTok is strengthening its commitment to enhancing platform safety features through the TikTok Community Guidelines. TikTok Malaysia public policy manager Aliff Zakaria said TikTok is also collaborating with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission to ensure content published complies with national laws and respects local cultural sensitivities. “To balance screen time and address gadget addiction issues, TikTok also empowers parents through the Family Pairing feature,” he said.

items.title: Federal Court fixes June 26 to hear Isa Samad’s conviction‑review application
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — The Federal Court has fixed June 26 to hear former Felda chairman Tan Sri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad’s application seeking leave to review an earlier decision reinstating his corruption conviction, six-year prison sentence and RM15.45 million fine. Mohd Isa’s lawyers sought the CRT to determine whether the earlier Federal Court panel had invited the defence to submit arguments on the merits of the case and mitigation after Mohd Isa was found guilty. Mohd Isa, 76, is currently serving his six-year prison sentence after the Federal Court on February 10 this year allowed the prosecution’s appeal, overturning his acquittal and reinstating his conviction, jail sentence and fine.

items.title: Social media users in Malaysia must verify age with using IC or passport from June 1, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUCHING, May 22 — Users of social media platforms will need to upload official government-issued documents for age verification following the implementation of two new codes under the Online Safety Act (ONSA) introduced by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), effective June 1. “We have now asked platforms to implement age verification. Teo said MCMC, however, had not prescribed any specific technology that platforms must use for the age verification process.

items.title: Johor police arrest six men over fatal slashing of 22‑year‑old in Pekan Nanas
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 22 — Johor police have arrested six local men to assist investigations into the murder of a 22-year-old man believed to have been slashed to death in Pekan Nanas, Pontian yesterday. Acting Johor police chief DCP Hoo Chuan Huat said the suspects were arrested in an operation conducted from midnight until early this morning at several locations around Johor Bahru. In a separate case, police arrested seven foreign men aged between 17 and 45 to assist investigations into the murder of a technician at an apartment in Prima Tebrau, Johor Bahru on May 13.

items.title: Higher Education Ministry: UPUOnline offers places to more than 169,000 SPM leavers for 2026 intake
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — A total of 169,803 students who sat for the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination have been offered places at public higher education institutions (IPTA) through UPUOnline. The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) said of the total, 91,357 candidates received offers from public universities, 60,947 from polytechnics, 15,844 from community colleges and 1,655 from institutions under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) Higher Education Division. “A total of 363 programmes were offered for this intake, comprising 41 certificate programmes, 57 foundation programmes, 264 diploma programmes and one bachelor’s degree programme.

items.title: Johor police bust drug lab, seize RM16.4m worth of narcotic‑laced vape cartridges
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 22 — Police busted an illegal drug processing lab that specialised in producing narcotic‑laced vape cartridges worth RM16.4 million were seized in three raids around Skudai and northern Johor Bahru recently. Acting Johor police chief Hoo Chuan Huat said those arrested were two locals and two foreigners. “Police raided premises in Taman Melawati and Taman Ungku Tun Aminah in Skudai that were used as a drug processing laboratory.

items.title: Only one new abandoned project recorded as delayed, sick private housing cases decline, says deputy housing minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — Statistics on private housing projects categorised as ‘delayed’ and ‘sick’ in Peninsular Malaysia showed a declining trend, as of April 30 this year, compared with March, said Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu. She added that, since its establishment in 2023, TFST has successfully revived 1,576 private housing projects, involving 185,525 housing units, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion. Of the total, 131 projects were successfully restored from ‘sick’ to ‘active’ status, 1,410 ‘sick’ projects were fully completed and obtained the Certificate of Completion and Compliance (CCC), and 35 ‘abandoned’ projects were successfully revived, either through physical completion of the projects or via buyer settlement plans.

items.title: Fuziah reminds PKR MPs to abide by party decisions, uphold mandate
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 22 — PKR MPs have been reminded to remain committed to the party’s mandate in carrying out their duties as elected representatives. PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said every elected representative must abide by party decisions and uphold the principles of the struggle entrusted to them. She said several party leaders had been tasked with engaging and advising the MPs involved on their responsibilities in carrying the party’s mandate.

items.title: Domestic Trade Ministry enforces nine‑day Kaamatan‑Gawai price‑control scheme in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan
items.summary: Armizan said for the 2026 Kaamatan Festival SHMMP in Sabah, a total of 12 types of goods are listed compared to 10 types last year with the addition of imported ginger and potatoes from China. “The items involved are live pigs, pork (meat and fat), pork (belly), imported buffalo meat from India, chicken wings, curly dried chillies, small shallots from India, garlic from China, large yellow onions, large shallots, imported aged ginger and potatoes from China. “For Labuan, 11 types of items are listed, namely pork (meat and fat), pork (belly), imported buffalo meat from India, chicken wings, curly dried chillies, small shallots from India, garlic from China, large yellow onions, large shallots, imported aged ginger and potatoes from China,” he said.

items.title: LLM forecasts 3.3 million vehicles on highways amid Aidiladha, school holidays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — The Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) expects the average daily traffic volume on major expressways and connecting highways to reach 3.3 million vehicles on peak days in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidiladha, which will be celebrated on May 27. It said the PLUS highway network is projected to record the highest traffic volume at 2.2 million vehicles, followed by the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) with 283,000 vehicles and the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK) with 209,000 vehicles. LLM would also be activating 34 Smart Lane locations, including along the PLUS highway network, while ensuring that toll collection systems function efficiently, including staggered toll collection at critical toll plazas.

items.title: Man gets 30 years, 9 strokes for incest with now-pregnant underage niece in Kanowit
items.summary: SIBU, May 22 — The Sessions Court here sentenced a 23-year-old man to a total of 30 years’ jail and nine strokes of the cane for committing incest with his 14-year-old niece. Judge Musyiri Peet imposed a sentence of 10 years’ jail and three strokes of the cane for each of the three charges under Section 376B(1) of the Penal Code, and ordered the imprisonment to run consecutively. The accused was charged with committing incest with his niece on three occasions, namely at around 2am on June 1, 2025 and around 11pm on June 2, 2025, and at around 8.30am on February 13, 2026.

items.title: Viral Bukit Bintang flare stunt ends with fines for clothing brand founder, six others
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Seven men, including the founder of a local clothing brand, were fined by the magistrates’ courts here today after pleading guilty to a string of offences linked to a viral publicity stunt involving flares and reckless riding in Bukit Bintang last weekend. According to the New Straits Times , the group admitted to various charges before three separate magistrates linked to the early morning stunt at the busy Jalan Sultan Ismail-Jalan Bukit Bintang junction on May 18. Five others — Amir Adzim Shahrul Nizam, 25; Khairul Hamizan Zainudin, 28; Muhammad Izz Mifzal Mohd Noordzlie, 22; Muhammad Ariff Aiman Mohamed Nasir, 23; and Muhammad Ikhwan Daniel Azman, 24 — admitted to committing public nuisance at the so-called “Shibuya Junction” area in Bukit Bintang around 2am that same day.

items.title: Selangor launches RM20m scheme to ease university fees for state‑born students, says Amirudin
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 22 — The Selangor state government has allocated RM20 million to cover half of the tuition fees for 4,000 Selangor-born students pursuing their studies at three state-owned higher education institutions. Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the three institutions are Universiti Islam Selangor (UIS), Universiti Selangor (UNISEL), and Selangor Technical Skills Development Centre (STDC). He said this at the Selangor People’s Tuition (PTRS) and Didik Kasih (PDK) 2026 programme in Section 7, here today.

items.title: ‘RM300 may seem small, but it’s hard-earned’: Ipoh budget rice stall robbery victim still traumatised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — A victim of a robbery at a budget rice stall in Lebuh Meru Raya, Ipoh, yesterday said she remains traumatised by the incident. Nor Atheerah Nadzirah Mior Ikram Shah, 21, said she has become easily startled and is now afraid to be alone following the ordeal. She said two men arrived on a motorcycle and stopped at the stall before one of them pretended to place a takeaway food order.

items.title: MCMC: Failure to comply with Online Safety Act’s risk‑mitigation code may incur fines up to RM10m
items.summary: According to the document, the RMC sets out the steps that must be taken by online service providers under Section 13 of the ONSA to reduce the risk of consumers being exposed to harmful online content on their services. “This includes assessing the risk of harmful content and implementing measures to mitigate that risk, thereby ensuring that their services are safe for users. Under the RMC, the harmful content refers to child sexual abuse material (CSAM), financial fraud, obscene and indecent content, as well as content that may cause harassment or distress.

items.title: Bersatu says issues raised by Hadi will be reviewed at leadership meetings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia today said it will discuss and scrutinise issues raised by PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang concerning ties between the two parties within Perikatan Nasional (PN). In a brief statement, Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the matters raised should be examined comprehensively “in the spirit of camaraderie, mutual understanding and shared responsibility” to strengthen unity within the coalition. Earlier today, Hadi said PAS was reviewing its political relationship and cooperation with Bersatu ahead of the 16th general election, citing several disagreements within Perikatan Nasional.

items.title: ‘Forgot’ and ‘rarely used’ excuses won’t fly as Penang JPJ seizes RM14m worth of luxury cars
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, May 22 — The Penang Road Transport Department (JPJ) has seized 51 luxury vehicles worth RM14 million for various offences related to Motor Vehicle Licences (LKM), or road tax, through a special operation dubbed ‘Ops Luxury’ conducted across the state from early January until yesterday. State JPJ director Zulkifly Ismail said a total of 124 actions or offences were recorded against owners of luxury vehicles who failed to renew their respective road tax. “The operation also detected a Ford Mustang that had not renewed its road tax since 2022, with the vehicle’s annual road tax estimated at RM10,900.

items.title: High Court dismisses Mahmud Abu Bekir Taib’s objection against Cahya Mata injunction, citing lack of proper notice
items.summary: KUCHING, May 22 — The High Court here has dismissed Dato Sri Mahmud Abu Bekir Taib’s preliminary objection against an ex parte injunction obtained by Cahya Mata Sarawak Berhad (Cahya Mata) and nine other parties. “No supporting affidavit or affidavit in opposition was filed. The court ordered Bekir to file and serve his affidavit in opposition on or before June 8, while Cahya Mata is to file and serve its affidavit in reply, if any, on or before June 29.

items.title: Police seek man with past murder record over alleged assault, death threats in Ranau
items.summary: RANAU, May 22 — Police are tracking down a 35-year-old man with a previous murder case record following an assault and criminal intimidation incident at a grocery store in Kampung Matupang here on Tuesday. District police chief Supt. Mursalin said the suspect allegedly threatened the victim before punching and kicking him repeatedly, causing injuries to the victim’s lip, shoulder and back, and the victim later sought medical treatment.
